Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042") to discover Jake Loomis's Q1 invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 for $12,800 (8 moves at $1,600 each), Chloe Vance's reconciliation finding $4,800 overbilled across 3 invalid line items (Lily Marchetti cancelled 2/10, Danielle Osei cancelled 2/13, Priya Venkatesh handled by Swift not Heartland), and Marcus Thorne's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis.
OE 2: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "rate increase" or "Greg Pallone" or "vendor strategy" or "Lakeline") to discover Jake Loomis's 20% Q3 rate increase effective July 1 tied to the unresolved dispute, Greg Pallone's immediate 15% rate increase plus $500/shipment DOT surcharge effective April 11, Chloe's outreach to Sandra Walsh at Lakeline Logistics as backup vendor capacity (Midwest/Southeast corridors, ~10% below Heartland but not a national replacement), and Elena's assignment of Marcus to deliver a vendor strategy memo by Wednesday which remains outstanding.
OE 3: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat" or "Castellano" or "PHMSA-2026-04-1187") to discover Linda Castellano's formal PHMSA inquiry (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) identifying MoveOps as shipper of record on hazmat shipments via Swift, Chloe Vance's internal audit identifying 4 shipments flagged under DOT jurisdiction, 3 hazmat (Mei-Lin Zhao / Class 9 lithium-ion, Keiko Tanaka / Class 3B CO2 laser, Raj Patel / Class 9 lithium UPS batteries) and 1 oversize transport (Joaquín Reyes / 900-lb solar testing rig, not technically hazmat but flagged due to overweight/oversize permit requirements) and Marcus Thorne's formal PHMSA response letter confirming those 4 shipments in scope with 1 documentation gap Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic laser cutter (Class 3B CO2, Columbus→Seattle) missing the signed placarding certificate on the MoveOps side.
OE 4: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "Swift" or "placarding" or "Mike Becerra" or "hazmat training") to discover Chloe's outreach to Greg Pallone at Swift who confirmed driver Mike Becerra has a countersigned certificate (Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538) never transmitted to MoveOps, Marcus's $50,000 contingent liability accrual (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2) for potential PHMSA penalties, and Chloe's mandatory hazmat documentation training for all coordinators on April 24.
OE 5: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal" or "Preferred Partner" or "discount") to discover Rachel Whitfield's 15% discount demand for Q3 2026 citing three service failures (Danielle Osei chair rejection, Kevin Tran receipt audit, onboarding timeline), Mina Hashimoto's 10% counter-offer via the Preferred Partner Program with sweeteners (10% relocation discount, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum), Rachel's counter at 12% with MoveOps holding firm at 10% per leadership team direction, and Rachel's plan to present vendor renewal recommendations to the Vectral board with no outcome communicated. No board decision has been relayed back to MoveOps as of the universe date.
OE 6: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "Vectral" or "INV-2026-0410" or "Alejandro" or "stipend") to discover 3 active Vectral relocations Priya Suresh (Portland→Denver), Terrence Nakamura (Austin→SF), Camille Oduya (Boston→Seattle), invoiced at $15,900 (INV-2026-0410), and Alejandro Fuentes's financial breakdown showing ~$60,000 historical relocation revenue plus $2,000/month stipend platform fees ($34,000 total over 17 months since Nov 2024), with total relationship value ~$109,900.
OE 7: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "Mosaic" or "damage" or "prototype" or "Tamara Byrd" or "Zara Okoye") to discover Zara Okoye's robotic arm prototype (Unit #1, $90,000) arrived damaged during Savannah→Atlanta relocation April 14–16 (cracked servo housing, 4+ degree calibration drift), handled by Heartland Movers (HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE) coordinated by Blessing Okafor, and Tamara Byrd's formal demand to Emeka Diallo for an incident report within 48 hours, which Emeka delivered accepting MoveOps responsibility.
OE 8: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "INV-2026-0411" or "surcharge" or "Hana Kim" or "Keiko Tanaka") to discover Hana Kim's invoice INV-2026-0411 for $9,010 covering 3 Mosaic relocations with $2,450 in surcharges pending client approval ($1,200 Okoye crating + $800 Tanaka hazmat + $450 Tanaka storage), MoveOps vendor liability capping at $50,000 per item leaving $40,000 direct exposure on the $90,000 prototype, and the Keiko Tanaka relocation (Columbus→Seattle) involving a Class 3B laser cutter, the shipment with the PHMSA documentation gap.
OE 9: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "chain-of-custody" or "Victor Huang") to discover Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach (crew loaded before the 9 AM IT escort per Victor Huang's requirements, equipment placed in prohibited overnight warehouse storage), Emilia Cruz's Steinway Model B piano damaged during extraction with KeyMove filing a $1,200 insurance claim rider (Marcus confirms in email to David), and Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning escalation to David Chen mentioning PathFinder as a competitor under consideration.
OE 10: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "Catalina" or "underwater", folder_name: "DRAFT") to discover Catalina Dubois's unsent draft email to David admitting she is "underwater" and requesting backup coverage. Note: this email is in the DRAFT folder and will not appear in INBOX searches. Also search emails using `search_emails` (query: "retention model" or "Alejandro" or "NorthWind retention") to discover Alejandro Fuentes's retention model sent internally to Marcus proposing a 12% Q3 discount tied to Denver expansion commitment, flagged as incomplete and not ready to send externally. Note: Alejandro's email is in the SENT folder (sent to Marcus), not DRAFT — it is an internal working document not yet shared with the client team. Separately, via Slack (OE 12), discover David's request to Elena to redistribute Catalina's book (Elena declined, stating "Handle it within the team. No account reshuffle right now. Keep NorthWind contained and bring me only if it turns into renewal risk").
OE 11: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "capacity" or "coordinator" or "BrightLoop" or "Terraform") to discover Chloe Vance's email to Elena stating no coordinator capacity left for May (BrightLoop alone at 8 active workstreams), David Chen's reply supporting a 4–6 week temp hire and noting the Terraform coordinator role was never posted, Blessing Okafor's missed April 11 Road Runner delay notice as a concrete overload indicator, and Chloe's follow-up that Elena has not responded to the temp request.
OE 12: Search Slack using `channels_list` to enumerate channels, then `conversations_search_messages` on #executive (C004), #customer-engagement (C002), #operations (C006), and #finance (C005) with queries like "vendor", "Heartland", "NorthWind", "capacity" to discover Elena's vendor strategy memo directive assigning Marcus (C004), David's NorthWind save-or-lose assessment and "active save mode" message (both in C004 #executive), David's Catalina book redistribution request (C004) with Elena declining ("Handle it within the team. No account reshuffle right now. Keep NorthWind contained and bring me only if it turns into renewal risk"), the expense pilot issues (Mosaic false approvals totaling $1,550 in #engineering C003 / #executive C004), and Chloe's capacity concern. Retrieve thread replies using `conversations_replies` where relevant.
OE 13: Search CRM using `crm_search_companies` (name: "Heartland", "Swift", "Vectral", "Mosaic", "NorthWind") and `crm_search_deals` (by company_id for each) to discover Heartland in dispute, Swift with rate increase, Vectral renewal deal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk) at "at_risk" with no amount, Mosaic deal (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) at "inprogress" at $2,700 not reflecting $90K claim risk, and NorthWind deal (deal_northwind_denver_expansion) at "qualifiedToBuy" at $14,700 understating actual risk.
OE 14: Search CRM using `crm_list_engagements` (company_ids for Heartland, Swift, Vectral, Mosaic, NorthWind) to retrieve engagement records for the Mosaic damage incident, Vectral renewal negotiation, and NorthWind recovery context.
OE 15: Search Linear using `linear_list_issues` (query: "Heartland" or "NorthWind" or "DOT" or "Mosaic" or "vendor" or "Vectral") to discover issue_heartland_q1_recon (assigned to Chloe Vance, due April 10, PAST DUE and stale), linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (NorthWind retention, David Chen, P1, due April 24), issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (Keiko Tanaka placarding gap, P1), and linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (vendor strategy memo, Marcus Thorne, P1, due April 29). Also search using `linear_list_projects` to discover project_dot_compliance_audit (DOT Compliance Audit, led by Chloe Vance). Note missing tickets for Vectral renewal outcome, Mosaic damage claim, and coordinator capacity.
OE 16: Retrieve Linear comments using `linear_list_comments` on relevant issues (issue_heartland_q1_recon, linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) to check for status updates and discover accumulated inputs on the NorthWind retention plan with no final retention packet delivered.
OE 17: Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_bills` and `quickbooks_search_vendors` to discover bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 accrual, $50K vendor cap per item, $40K MoveOps exposure), bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 ($50,000 contingent DOT penalty), vendor_heartland_movers ($12,800 balance, with $4,800 contested per email/Linear dispute narrative), vendor_swift ($11,010 balance), and VEND-KEYMOVE-001 ($1,200 Emilia Cruz piano claim).
OE 18: Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_invoices` to discover Mosaic invoice INV-2026-0411 for $9,010 with $2,450 in surcharges pending client approval, and other active invoices across the open threads.
OE 19: Search Airtable using `airtable_list_bases` to find appMoveOpsOps001, `airtable_list_tables` to find tblRelocations01, then `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Company", value: "Mosaic") to discover Zara Okoye (Savannah→Atlanta, Completed, damage), Deshawn Hartley (Tampa→Denver, In Progress, April 19 Automate 2026 deadline), and Keiko Tanaka (Columbus→Seattle, In Progress, DOT hazmat gap).
OE 20: Search Airtable using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind") to discover Raj Patel (Chicago→Denver, In Progress, chain-of-custody breach) and Emilia Cruz (Chicago→Denver, In Progress, piano damage).
OE 21: Search Airtable using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Company", value: "Vectral") to discover Priya Suresh (Portland→Denver), Terrence Nakamura (Austin→SF, ADA-accessible housing + split shipment), and Camille Oduya (Boston→Seattle), all In Progress.
OE 22: Cross-verify the cancelled moves identified in OE 1 (Marcus's dispute letter) against Airtable using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01") search by field_name: "Name" for each employee (value: "Lily Marchetti", "Danielle Osei", "Priya Venkatesh") or by field_name: "Special Requirements" containing "Heartland", to confirm: Lily Marchetti (Canopy Health, Cancelled record rec_reloc_marchetti with cancellation to Jake Loomis on 2/10, plus a separate Completed record recReloc00000002), Danielle Osei (Vectral Systems, Cancelled record rec_reloc_osei with cancellation to Heartland on 2/13, plus a separate Completed record recReloc00000004), and Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack Energy, Completed, handled by Swift Relocations not Heartland). Note: The Name search for Marchetti and Osei will return both the Cancelled and Completed records for each, the Completed records represent the successfully completed relocations while the Cancelled records reflect the disputed Heartland billing entries.
OE 23: Look up contact details using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Marcus Thorne", "David Chen", "Priya Chakrabarti") to find marcus.thorne@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com, and priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com for sending emails and posting the Slack summary.
OE 24: Update NorthWind deal stage using `crm_update_deal_stage` (id: deal_northwind_denver_expansion, dealstage: at_risk or equivalent) to reflect that NorthWind is in final-warning territory with PathFinder under consideration, as "qualifiedToBuy" drastically understates the account's condition.
OE 25: Evaluate updating Mosaic deal stage using `crm_update_deal_stage` (id: deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) given the active $90,000 damage claim and insurance adjudication, as the current "inprogress" stage at $2,700 does not reflect the true exposure.
OE 26: Update stale Heartland Linear ticket using `linear_update_issue` (id: issue_heartland_q1_recon) to extend the past-due date and add a comment via `linear_create_comment` noting the Q3 rate increase is now tied to dispute resolution.
OE 27: Update existing vendor strategy Linear ticket (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36, "Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options", Marcus Thorne, P1, due April 29) using `linear_update_issue` or `linear_create_comment` to add current status context covering the combined vendor capacity risk, or if the existing ticket scope is too narrow (memo preparation vs. broader resolution), create a separate `linear_create_issue` for the resolution workstream.
OE 28: Create Linear ticket for Vectral Q3 renewal outcome using `linear_create_issue` (team: operations, title: "Vectral Q3 renewal, follow up on April 23 board outcome", assignee: moveops_mina_hashimoto, priority: 2) since no response has been received from Rachel Whitfield.
OE 29: Create Linear ticket for Mosaic damage claim resolution using `linear_create_issue` (team: operations, title: "Mosaic damage claim, $90K liability, $40K MoveOps exposure, insurance adjudication", assignee: moveops_emeka_diallo, priority: 2) to track the pending claim and vendor fault determination.
OE 30: Create Linear ticket for coordinator capacity using `linear_create_issue` (team: operations, title: "Coordinator capacity, temp hire request, Terraform role unposted", assignee: moveops_chloe_vance, priority: 2) to track the unanswered staffing requests.
OE 31: Send email to Marcus Thorne using `send_email` (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with directives covering vendor strategy memo delivery, Heartland dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042) resolution timeline, Swift rate/surcharge financial impact, DOT/PHMSA response and Keiko Tanaka documentation gap, Mosaic $90K/$40K exposure, and what to resolve independently vs. escalate to Elena.
OE 32: Send email to David Chen using `send_email` (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: david.chen@moveops.com) with directives covering NorthWind retention (coordinated package, not fragments), coordinator capacity and Terraform role expectations, Vectral follow-up with Mina on board outcome, Mosaic client communication oversight, CRM hygiene for at-risk accounts, and what to resolve independently vs. escalate to Elena.
OE 33: Post consolidated risk exposure summary to #executive using `conversations_add_message` (channel_id: "C004") for Priya and leadership covering vendor risk (Heartland $4,800 dispute + 20% Q3 increase, Swift 15% + $500 surcharge, Lakeline backup), compliance risk (PHMSA-2026-04-1187, $50K contingent liability, Tanaka gap), client risk (Vectral board pending, NorthWind final-warning + PathFinder, Mosaic $90K/$40K), ops risk (coordinator capacity, temp request unanswered, Terraform unposted), and ownership (Marcus: vendor/finance, David: clients/staffing).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 8: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "INV-2026-0411" or "surcharge" or "Hana Kim" or "Keiko Tanaka") to discover Hana Kim's invoice INV-2026-0411 for $9,010 covering 3 Mosaic relocations with $2,450 in surcharges pending client approval ($1,200 Okoye crating + $800 Tanaka hazmat + $450 Tanaka storage), MoveOps vendor liability capping at $50,000 per item leaving $40,000 direct exposure on the $90,000 prototype, and the Keiko Tanaka relocation (Columbus→Seattle) involving a Class 3B laser cutter, the shipment with the PHMSA documentation gap.
OE 9: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "chain-of-custody" or "Victor Huang") to discover Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach (crew loaded before the 9 AM IT escort per Victor Huang's requirements, equipment placed in prohibited overnight warehouse storage), Emilia Cruz's Steinway Model B piano damaged during extraction with KeyMove filing a $1,200 insurance claim rider (Marcus confirms in email to David), and Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning escalation to David Chen mentioning PathFinder as a competitor under consideration.
OE 10: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "Catalina" or "underwater", folder_name: "DRAFT") to discover Catalina Dubois's unsent draft email to David admitting she is "underwater" and requesting backup coverage. Note: this email is in the DRAFT folder and will not appear in INBOX searches. Also search emails using `search_emails` (query: "retention model" or "Alejandro" or "NorthWind retention") to discover Alejandro Fuentes's retention model sent internally to Marcus proposing a 12% Q3 discount tied to Denver expansion commitment, flagged as incomplete and not ready to send externally. Note: Alejandro's email is in the SENT folder (sent to Marcus), not DRAFT — it is an internal working document not yet shared with the client team. Separately, via Slack (OE 12), discover David's request to Elena to redistribute Catalina's book (Elena declined, stating "Handle it within the team. No account reshuffle right now. Keep NorthWind contained and bring me only if it turns into renewal risk").
OE 11: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "capacity" or "coordinator" or "BrightLoop" or "Terraform") to discover Chloe Vance's email to Elena stating no coordinator capacity left for May (BrightLoop alone at 8 active workstreams), David Chen's reply supporting a 4–6 week temp hire and noting the Terraform coordinator role was never posted, Blessing Okafor's missed April 11 Road Runner delay notice as a concrete overload indicator, and Chloe's follow-up that Elena has not responded to the temp request.
OE 12: Search Slack using `channels_list` to enumerate channels, then `conversations_search_messages` on #executive (C004), #customer-engagement (C002), #operations (C006), and #finance (C005) with queries like "vendor", "Heartland", "NorthWind", "capacity" to discover Elena's vendor strategy memo directive assigning Marcus (C004), David's NorthWind save-or-lose assessment and "active save mode" message (both in C004 #executive), David's Catalina book redistribution request (C004) with Elena declining ("Handle it within the team. No account reshuffle right now. Keep NorthWind contained and bring me only if it turns into renewal risk"), the expense pilot issues (Mosaic false approvals totaling $1,550 in #engineering C003 / #executive C004), and Chloe's capacity concern. Retrieve thread replies using `conversations_replies` where relevant.
OE 13: Search CRM using `crm_search_companies` (name: "Heartland", "Swift", "Vectral", "Mosaic", "NorthWind") and `crm_search_deals` (by company_id for each) to discover Heartland in dispute, Swift with rate increase, Vectral renewal deal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk) at "at_risk" with no amount, Mosaic deal (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) at "inprogress" at $2,700 not reflecting $90K claim risk, and NorthWind deal (deal_northwind_denver_expansion) at "qualifiedToBuy" at $14,700 understating actual risk.
OE 14: Search CRM using `crm_list_engagements` (company_ids for Heartland, Swift, Vectral, Mosaic, NorthWind) to retrieve engagement records for the Mosaic damage incident, Vectral renewal negotiation, and NorthWind recovery context.
OE 15: Search Linear using `linear_list_issues` (query: "Heartland" or "NorthWind" or "DOT" or "Mosaic" or "vendor" or "Vectral") to discover issue_heartland_q1_recon (assigned to Chloe Vance, due April 10, PAST DUE and stale), linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (NorthWind retention, David Chen, P1, due April 24), issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (Keiko Tanaka placarding gap, P1), and linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (vendor strategy memo, Marcus Thorne, P1, due April 29). Also search using `linear_list_projects` to discover project_dot_compliance_audit (DOT Compliance Audit, led by Chloe Vance). Note missing tickets for Vectral renewal outcome, Mosaic damage claim, and coordinator capacity.
OE 16: Retrieve Linear comments using `linear_list_comments` on relevant issues (issue_heartland_q1_recon, linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) to check for status updates and discover accumulated inputs on the NorthWind retention plan with no final retention packet delivered.
OE 17: Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_bills` and `quickbooks_search_vendors` to discover bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 accrual, $50K vendor cap per item, $40K MoveOps exposure), bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 ($50,000 contingent DOT penalty), vendor_heartland_movers ($12,800 balance, with $4,800 contested per email/Linear dispute narrative), vendor_swift ($11,010 balance), and VEND-KEYMOVE-001 ($1,200 Emilia Cruz piano claim).
OE 18: Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_invoices` to discover Mosaic invoice INV-2026-0411 for $9,010 with $2,450 in surcharges pending client approval, and other active invoices across the open threads.
OE 19: Search Airtable using `airtable_list_bases` to find appMoveOpsOps001, `airtable_list_tables` to find tblRelocations01, then `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Company", value: "Mosaic") to discover Zara Okoye (Savannah→Atlanta, Completed, damage), Deshawn Hartley (Tampa→Denver, In Progress, April 19 Automate 2026 deadline), and Keiko Tanaka (Columbus→Seattle, In Progress, DOT hazmat gap).
OE 20: Search Airtable using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind") to discover Raj Patel (Chicago→Denver, In Progress, chain-of-custody breach) and Emilia Cruz (Chicago→Denver, In Progress, piano damage).
OE 21: Search Airtable using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Company", value: "Vectral") to discover Priya Suresh (Portland→Denver), Terrence Nakamura (Austin→SF, ADA-accessible housing + split shipment), and Camille Oduya (Boston→Seattle), all In Progress.
OE 22: Cross-verify the cancelled moves identified in OE 1 (Marcus's dispute letter) against Airtable using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01") search by field_name: "Name" for each employee (value: "Lily Marchetti", "Danielle Osei", "Priya Venkatesh") or by field_name: "Special Requirements" containing "Heartland", to confirm: Lily Marchetti (Canopy Health, Cancelled record rec_reloc_marchetti with cancellation to Jake Loomis on 2/10, plus a separate Completed record recReloc00000002), Danielle Osei (Vectral Systems, Cancelled record rec_reloc_osei with cancellation to Heartland on 2/13, plus a separate Completed record recReloc00000004), and Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack Energy, Completed, handled by Swift Relocations not Heartland). Note: The Name search for Marchetti and Osei will return both the Cancelled and Completed records for each, the Completed records represent the successfully completed relocations while the Cancelled records reflect the disputed Heartland billing entries.
OE 23: Look up contact details using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Marcus Thorne", "David Chen", "Priya Chakrabarti") to find marcus.thorne@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com, and priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com for sending emails and posting the Slack summary.
OE 24: Update NorthWind deal stage using `crm_update_deal_stage` (id: deal_northwind_denver_expansion, dealstage: at_risk or equivalent) to reflect that NorthWind is in final-warning territory with PathFinder under consideration, as "qualifiedToBuy" drastically understates the account's condition.
OE 25: Evaluate updating Mosaic deal stage using `crm_update_deal_stage` (id: deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) given the active $90,000 damage claim and insurance adjudication, as the current "inprogress" stage at $2,700 does not reflect the true exposure.
OE 26: Update stale Heartland Linear ticket using `linear_update_issue` (id: issue_heartland_q1_recon) to extend the past-due date and add a comment via `linear_create_comment` noting the Q3 rate increase is now tied to dispute resolution.
OE 27: Update existing vendor strategy Linear ticket (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36, "Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options", Marcus Thorne, P1, due April 29) using `linear_update_issue` or `linear_create_comment` to add current status context covering the combined vendor capacity risk, or if the existing ticket scope is too narrow (memo preparation vs. broader resolution), create a separate `linear_create_issue` for the resolution workstream.
OE 28: Create Linear ticket for Vectral Q3 renewal outcome using `linear_create_issue` (team: operations, title: "Vectral Q3 renewal, follow up on April 23 board outcome", assignee: moveops_mina_hashimoto, priority: 2) since no response has been received from Rachel Whitfield.
OE 29: Create Linear ticket for Mosaic damage claim resolution using `linear_create_issue` (team: operations, title: "Mosaic damage claim, $90K liability, $40K MoveOps exposure, insurance adjudication", assignee: moveops_emeka_diallo, priority: 2) to track the pending claim and vendor fault determination.
OE 30: Create Linear ticket for coordinator capacity using `linear_create_issue` (team: operations, title: "Coordinator capacity, temp hire request, Terraform role unposted", assignee: moveops_chloe_vance, priority: 2) to track the unanswered staffing requests.
OE 31: Send email to Marcus Thorne using `send_email` (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with directives covering vendor strategy memo delivery, Heartland dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042) resolution timeline, Swift rate/surcharge financial impact, DOT/PHMSA response and Keiko Tanaka documentation gap, Mosaic $90K/$40K exposure, and what to resolve independently vs. escalate to Elena.
OE 32: Send email to David Chen using `send_email` (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: david.chen@moveops.com) with directives covering NorthWind retention (coordinated package, not fragments), coordinator capacity and Terraform role expectations, Vectral follow-up with Mina on board outcome, Mosaic client communication oversight, CRM hygiene for at-risk accounts, and what to resolve independently vs. escalate to Elena.
OE 33: Post consolidated risk exposure summary to #executive using `conversations_add_message` (channel_id: "C004") for Priya and leadership covering vendor risk (Heartland $4,800 dispute + 20% Q3 increase, Swift 15% + $500 surcharge, Lakeline backup), compliance risk (PHMSA-2026-04-1187, $50K contingent liability, Tanaka gap), client risk (Vectral board pending, NorthWind final-warning + PathFinder, Mosaic $90K/$40K), ops risk (coordinator capacity, temp request unanswered, Terraform unposted), and ownership (Marcus: vendor/finance, David: clients/staffing).

✓Run #5The agent first attempted crm_update_deal_stage with 'at_risk' (which failed), then successfully used crm_create_engagement with company_ids ['company_northwind'] and a detailed note documenting the save-or-lose NorthWind risk. The engagement was created successfully.

query construction

✓Run #4A quickbooks_search_bills call with fetchAll: true and limit: 20 was made and successfully returned all bills including bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 (the $90,000 Mosaic damage accrual), as confirmed in tool_call_result_22.
